name: Re-engagement Segment Builder
description: Governed 5-step re-engagement engine. Dispatches to n8n to segment old followers into warm/cool/cold tiers by engagement recency, PII-audits all returned rows, writes approved=false segment proposals to manifests/staging/, and surfaces a human review deck for operator action before any calendar dispatch fires. Warm segment requires mandatory operator caption review before promotion.

platform.description: Platform to segment. Enum: tiktok | instagram | email.

brand.description: Brand to segment. Enum: discobass | steelezone.

lookback_days.description: Days of engagement history to analyse. Must be 1–180. Segments: warm=30–60d, cool=60–90d, cold=90–180d.

trigger_reason.description: Why this segment build is being triggered. Non-empty; logged for audit trail.

segments_path.description: Path to the staged segment file written to manifests/staging/segments_<brand>_<date>.json.

segment_counts.description: Counts per tier: {warm: N, cool: N, cold: N}.

pii_audit_verdict.description: pass or halt — PII audit decision across all returned segment rows.

review_deck_path.description: Path to the operator review deck written to reports/.

steps.name: Validate workflow inputs
steps.description: Audit Agent validates all inputs before any dispatch: platform must be one of tiktok|instagram|email, brand must be one of discobass|steelezone, lookback_days must be an integer in [1, 180], trigger_reason must be non-empty. Any validation failure halts immediately — no n8n dispatch fires on invalid inputs.

safety.description: Pure input validation. No writes, no network calls. Invalid inputs halt the run before any data leaves the OS.

steps.name: Dispatch to n8n segment builder
steps.description: Workflow Agent dispatches to the content__reengage_segment_build n8n endpoint via http_post_n8n with payload keys: platform, brand, lookback_days, trigger_reason, n8n_run_id. Receives segment classification rows from n8n. Does not write rows to disk — rows are held in memory and passed to step 3 for PII audit. Only fires if step_1 audit_pre_check_passed=true.

safety.description: Dispatch step. http_post_n8n is the only permitted outbound tool — network_calls is explicitly denied. Segment rows are NOT written to disk here; they pass directly to step 3.

steps.name: PII audit on returned segment rows
steps.description: Audit Agent inspects every row returned by n8n. Checks: (1) no email address, phone number, full name, or partial name in any column; (2) user_id is an opaque platform identifier — numeric string for TikTok/IG, 64-char hex SHA-256 for email-platform users; (3) segment value is one of warm|cool|cold; (4) engagement_score is a float in [0.0, 1.0]; (5) approved field is present and defaults to false. Any PII detection or schema violation is a GovernanceError — the entire run halts and no rows are written to staging.

safety.description: PII gate. Any row containing PII triggers GovernanceError and halts the entire run. Audit verdict and violation details written to logs/ and reports/ for operator inspection.

steps.name: Write validated segment rows to manifests/staging/
steps.description: Manifest Agent writes all PII-clean segment rows to manifests/staging/segments_<brand>_<date>.json. Every row has approved set to false. The file embeds the CR reference (2026-03-18-reengagement-engine-phase-3) and the audit run ID from step 3. This file is not executable — no calendar dispatch or repost fires until the operator explicitly sets approved=true on selected rows and promotes the file.

safety.description: Writes segment rows to manifests/staging/ only. approved=false on all rows — no dispatch fires without operator action. Paid retargeting export is explicitly excluded from this workflow.

steps.name: Write operator review deck
steps.description: Reporting Agent writes a structured operator review deck to reports/reengagement_review_<brand>_<date>.json and a human-readable summary. Deck includes: segment counts by tier (warm/cool/cold), top-performing posts per tier, draft captions per brand (warm tier gets personalization prompt templates), 60-day repeat guard status per slot, approval instructions, and CR reference. Never logs PII, full URLs, or tokens.

safety.description: Writes only to logs/workflows/ and reports/. Review deck is the primary operator artifact — warm segment review and segment_promotion_to_calendar gates must be cleared before any calendar dispatch fires.
